title: gustavia nana subsp. nana
intro: Gustavia nana subsp. nana, also known as the Dwarf Guayabita or Dwarf Guayabita de Panama, is a small shrub native to Panama and Costa Rica, belonging to the family Hernandiaceae. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Gustavia nana subsp. nana include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antiparasitic agent. Herbal preparations of Gustavia nana subsp. nana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by boiling the leaves and stems in water or extracting the active compounds with a solvent.
